title: mildly sweet brazilian pea-eggplant toast
description: a sweet and tender brazilian dish made with peas and eggplant, served on toast. serves as a pescatarian-friendly breakfast or snack option.
instructions: preheat a tava or griddle over medium heat.
in a large bowl, whisk together eggs, honey, salt, and black pepper.
add the cubed eggplant to the bowl and toss to coat with the egg mixture.
heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
add the eggplant mixture to the skillet and cook until the eggplant is tender, about 8 minutes.
add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
add the frozen peas to the skillet and cook until thawed and tender, about 3 minutes.
toast the bread slices on the tava or griddle.
spread a layer of the eggplant mixture on each toasted bread slice.
drizzle with the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil and sprinkle with queso fresco.
garnish with chopped cilantro and serve immediately.
serve the toast with a fried egg on top, if desired.
